contextBlurb: Colorado has 7 relevant bills tracked in 2025–2026 — 2 on data-center policy and 5 on AI regulation. 3 impose active bans or moratoriums.

legislation.title: Large-Load Data Centers
legislation.summary: This bill establishes accountability measures for large-scale data centers in Colorado, likely including requirements around energy use, grid impact, and reporting. It aims to ensure that data centers consuming significant amounts of power are subject to oversight and transparency obligations.

legislation.title: Prohibit Use of Personally Identifying Feature
legislation.summary: This bill bans the use of someone's personal identifying features — such as their face, voice, or likeness — without their permission. It aims to protect individuals from unauthorized digital reproduction or impersonation of their identity.

legislation.title: Use of Surveillance Technology by Law Enforcement
legislation.summary: This bill regulates how law enforcement agencies in Colorado can use surveillance technologies, with the goal of protecting citizens' rights. It likely requires oversight, transparency, or restrictions on tools like facial recognition or other monitoring systems used by police.

legislation.title: Data Center & Utility Modernization
legislation.summary: This bill sets up a framework to guide how data centers are built and powered in Colorado, requiring them to support clean energy, grid modernization, and environmental protections. It aims to balance the rapid growth of data centers with the needs of utility customers and the environment.

legislation.title: Conversational Artificial Intelligence Service Operator Requirements
legislation.summary: This bill sets rules for companies that operate AI chatbot or conversational AI services, requiring them to meet certain transparency and safety standards. It likely includes protections for users, particularly around disclosure that they are interacting with an AI and safeguards for vulnerable users such as children.

legislation.title: Psychotherapy Artificial Intelligence Restrictions
legislation.summary: This bill places restrictions on how artificial intelligence can be used in psychotherapy services in Colorado. It aims to protect patients by limiting or regulating AI's role in mental health treatment.

legislation.title: Sunset Business Intelligence Center Advisory Board
legislation.summary: This bill dissolves an advisory board related to the Business Intelligence Center in Colorado based on a state agency review recommendation. It is an administrative sunset action and is not directly related to AI regulation or data-center policy.

legislation.title: Modifications to Elections
legislation.summary: This bill modifies Colorado election laws, likely including rules around the use of AI-generated or synthetic media in political campaigns. It addresses how deepfakes or digitally altered content may be used or disclosed in election-related communications.

legislation.title: Adding Municipal Jails to County Jail Oversight Requirements
legislation.summary: This bill extends existing oversight requirements for county jails to also cover municipal jails. It has no connection to AI regulation or data-center policy.

legislation.title: Law Enforcement National Electronic Tracing System & Share Program
legislation.summary: This bill deals with law enforcement agencies using the federal ATF's gun tracing system to track firearms. It is not related to AI or data-center policy.

legislation.title: Create Department of Personnel Program Pathways to Public Service
legislation.summary: This bill creates a public service pathways program within Colorado's Department of Personnel. It is not related to AI or data-center policy.

legislation.title: Capital Construction Information Technology
legislation.summary: This bill deals with funding and appropriations for government capital construction information technology projects. It is a budget/appropriations measure and is not substantively about AI regulation or data-center policy.
